disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Acute severe bilateral jaw pain with marked restriction of mouth opening (trismus) developing over 12 hours
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me more about the jaw pain and how much you can open your mouth?
What exactly is happening with your jaw right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The muscles around my jaw have seized up. It's a deep cramping ache both sides, up into my temples, and I can barely get my mouth open — just a tiny gap.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: History of chronic teeth grinding and clenching (bruxism), worse at night, with a recent intense episode
trigger_question_examples: Do you grind or clench your teeth, especially at night?
Have you been clenching your jaw a lot lately?
Do you have any history of teeth grinding or jaw clenching?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, I grind and clench my teeth a lot, mostly in my sleep. It's been worse lately with all the stress.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Widespread body ache and known fibromyalgia — background central sensitisation
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any pain or aches anywhere else in your body?
Do you have any ongoing health conditions?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I have fibromyalgia, so my whole body aches a lot of the time. Right now it feels flared up on top of the jaw pain.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Unable to drink adequately because of pain on opening the mouth — hydration at risk
trigger_question_examples: Are you able to drink enough fluids at the moment?
Can you swallow liquids okay, or is the pain stopping you drinking?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Not really — it hurts too much to open my mouth so I've barely had anything to drink today, which is worrying me.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No visual disturbance, no new scalp or temple tenderness, no jaw pain specifically on chewing that eases with rest (screening for giant cell arteritis)
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any changes to your vision — blurring, double vision or loss of sight?
Is your scalp or the area over your temples tender to touch?
Does the pain come on specifically when you chew and ease when you rest?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my vision is fine and my scalp isn't sore to touch. The pain is there all the time from the muscles seizing, not just when I chew.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I haven't really thought about my vision.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No spreading swelling under the chin or into the neck, no drooling, no difficulty breathing or swallowing (screening for odontogenic deep space infection)
trigger_question_examples: Is there any swelling under your chin, in your neck, or the floor of your mouth?
Are you having any trouble breathing or swallowing your saliva, or are you drooling?
Have you noticed any hot, spreading swelling in your face or neck?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, there's no swelling in my neck or under my chin, and I can breathe fine — it's just the muscles that are locked and sore.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever or systemic illness; feels distressed but not febrile
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fever, chills or felt generally unwell?
Do you feel hot or feverish at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever — I checked my temperature and it was normal. I just feel wound up and in a lot of pain.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Tetanus immunisation up to date; no recent penetrating wound; no generalised stiffness or muscle spasms elsewhere (screening for tetanus)
trigger_question_examples: Are your tetanus vaccinations up to date, and have you had any recent cuts or puncture wounds?
Do you have any stiffness or spasms in the rest of your body, like your back or neck arching?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My tetanus shots are up to date and I haven't cut myself on anything. It's just my jaw and my usual body aches, nothing arching or spasming elsewhere.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Simple over-the-counter analgesia (paracetamol and ibuprofen) has given no relief for the muscle spasm
trigger_question_examples: What have you already tried for the pain, and did it help?
Have you taken any painkillers today?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've taken paracetamol and ibuprofen today and they've done nothing at all for the spasm.
